description_long: This sculpture depicts two cherubic children standing closely together, entwined around a large, vertically oriented fish or aquatic creature. The children are rendered with rounded, youthful features and are partially draped, with one child appearing to hold onto the fish's body. The fish's mouth is open at the top, and its scales and fins are subtly detailed. The entire composition is mounted on a circular, dark base that contrasts with the weathered, stone-like texture of the figures. The surface shows signs of age and patina, suggesting it is made from stone or concrete. The overall form is compact and vertical, with intricate details visible from multiple angles.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of two cherubic children entwined around a large fish, mounted on a circular base. The figures are detailed and the surface has a rough, aged texture.
description_view_0: The front view shows both children and the fish clearly, with the open mouth of the fish at the top and the children embracing its body.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the narrow profile of the sculpture, emphasizing the verticality and the side of one child and the fish.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the backs of the children and the fish, with less facial detail but more of the fish's body visible.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the other child's profile and the curvature of the fish, showing the entwined composition from a different angle.
attribute_subject_matter: two children and a large fish
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture is isolated on a plain background.
